_meta.source: design/02-questions.md — our own question copy, replacing hello-stranger's race/age/politics/edu demographics.
_meta.principle: All options are 'state right now', not 'fixed attribute'. Users should feel OK re-picking any time.
changelog: v3: split 'color' into two independent dims — LOOK (sprite skeleton, 5 vars) + MOOD_COLOR (char tint, 5 vars). Plus POSE (3 vars) exposed as independent dim instead of internal animation frame.

v4: aligned to design/02-questions.md. Removed Q_SMILE (smile auto-triggered by mood.prefer_smile). Mood no longer drives color; it drives animation interval + smile toggle. Single color source: energy.hex → darken/lighten/darkest derives character/background/border colors.

v6: merged Q_LOOK + Q_POSE into a single Q_AVATAR visual gallery (15 thumbnails per sex). Rationale: in hello-stranger's original (HelloStranger.person.svelte:146-149 in github.com/the-pudding/hello-stranger) color was derived from race and num from personKey-hash — neither was ever user-facing. v5 mechanically exposed both underlying fields as two separate UI questions, which implied a composition semantics that doesn't exist (the 15 sprites per sex are independent hand-drawn illustrations, not a 5×3 composable matrix). v6 collapses them back into a single 'pick this one' gallery. No downstream change — renderer.js buildSpriteKey still reads sex_color_num[smile].

Q_S.prompt: Pick a body — which one feels like you today?

Q_AVATAR.prompt: Which avatar feels like you today?
Q_AVATAR.note: v6: replaces Q_LOOK + Q_POSE. Renders as a 15-tile visual gallery (5 × 3) per sex. Tiles are derived from sprites.json keys at runtime — no options array here. Click a tile to set state.color (0..4) and state.num (0..2) atomically. The 15 sprites are independent hand-drawn illustrations, NOT a composable look×pose matrix.

Q_SMILE.note: v5: smile is now an independent user dimension (was mood-derived in v4). Completes the 2×5×3×2=60 sprite combination matrix from hello-stranger. Falls back to neutral sprite if no smile variant exists for the chosen sex/look/pose.

Q_MOOD.prompt: How do you feel right now?
Q_MOOD.note: v5: mood is kept as a soft vibe tag, but no longer affects animation interval (that is now a hidden constant — see app.js ANIM_INTERVAL_MS). prefer_smile is also no longer applied automatically; smile is a direct user choice.

Q_ENERGY.note: Single color source. energy[bg_level].hex → darkenColor=character color, lightenColor=background, darkestColor=border. Same derivation algorithm as hello-stranger (MIT).

Q_INTERESTS.note: Multi-select tag picker. state.interests is an array of tag values. Icons are Unicode symbols chosen to work in both the browser font and Unifont on the device side (when firmware eventually renders them). Web-only for now — not in the device sync payload.
